content.text: Now the paddock rewrite — writing the gazette, then committing it via the setter:

input.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BZ

## Boundary — single tier, office impersonation (cinched 260612)

This heat builds the **one** operator credential model:
workforce federation with **office-SA impersonation**.
The keyfile citizen tier is **canceled unbuilt** —
no mode enum, no second tier, no migration machinery
(decision record: `Memos/memo-20260612-office-federation-conversion.md`;
canon banner updated same day).
Prerequisite ladder accepted: every manor brings a domain (org anchor, a deed not a building),
a Cloud Identity org, and a conformant external OIDC IdP.
Today's keyfile machinery is **bridge legacy**:
untouched, riding under the accessor seam, suites stay green on it
until federation personas pass the same suites —
then the RBRA estate retires whole as its own movement.
Scaffolding with a demolition condition, never a tier.

## The premise — human present (cinched; earmarked for RBS0)

Unchanged from the canon's D2:
**a human is present at the kickoff of every run; no run outlives a session cap.**
No refresh token anywhere beyond the payor's own;
an orchestrating agent never holds a secret
(device-flow kickoff surfaces only user_code + verification URI).
Belongs in RBS0 as a premise quoin voiced `axk_premise` at civic incorporation.

## The office architecture (cinched 260612)

**Capability-sets instantiate as office SAs at levy; all resource IAM freezes there.**

- Levy creates the office SAs (governor / director / retriever) beside mason
  and grants **every** resource binding (project, GAR repo, mason actAs) to them, once,
  behind a settle gate — levy is long and payor-driven; the gate is cheap there.
- Admission to an office is **one binding**:
  `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` on the office SA, naming the operator's `principal://` subject.
  Removal is that binding's deletion.
- Runtime chain: IdP device flow (Leg 1) → STS exchange (Leg 2) →
  `generateAccessToken` on the office SA (Leg 3) → short-lived office token.
  Everything downstream bearer-blind, unchanged.
- **Zero service account keys exist anywhere**; the depot runs with
  `disableServiceAccountKeyCreation` fully enforced.
  The system's sole durable secret is the payor's RBRO refresh token.
- No per-user Google identity ever exists:
  the federated principal is the grantable identity, implicit in the IdP assertion.
  The IdP is the census (cinch carried forward).
- Flap payoff (the architecture's origin): post-levy IAM mutation collapses to
  one binding type at one scope with one gate point;
  the SA-lifecycle flap family confines to levy;
  hot paths perform zero IAM mutation.
  Office tokens are uncapped and self-expiring; the 10-key cap is irrelevant (no keys).
- An impersonated office token is a plain SA token —
  the workforce federated-token per-product support matrix is dodged entirely.
- Blast radius: a token is one office at a time;
  the multi-hat union-credential concern dissolves.

## The civic structure (cinched, carried forward with office recast)

**Payor founds, governors populate, terriers tell, IAM enforces.**

- Admission authority scoped to the polity it admits into; payor outside the citizenry,
  ceremonial after founding; founding gestures gain **establish-federation**
  (workforce pool + provider + attribute mapping, org-level, once per manor)
  and **office establishment** at levy.
- All operator administration is governor-wielded and depot-scoped.
  A governor admits another to the governor office — governors create governors.
  Cross-depot administration does not exist.
- Governor dissolves structurally: an operator holding the governor office.
  Standing governor remains the default posture.
- One-identity-across-depots arrives free:
  the same `principal://` subject is grantable in every depot under the manor.

## Multi-IdP posture (cinched 260612)

One live provider per pool is the norm.
Subjects are **pool-scoped, not provider-scoped** —
every provider is a full root of trust for the whole subject namespace,
so the pool's security floor is its weakest provider.
Adding a provider is a payor ceremony with founding gravity.
Dual-provider windows exist for IdP **migration** only (overlap, re-admit, retire) —
never as an availability hedge; live tokens already ride out IdP outages within the session cap.

## The Terrier (carried forward; entries recast)

Per-polity terriers in the Manor-homed Terrier bucket, managed-folder grain,
payor-created at levy, governor-writeable own-terrier-only,
read population governors-and-above — all unchanged.
Entries now record (principal subject, office held);
key IDs vanish with the keys.
Working verbs never touch terriers; audit is a read-only diff
(terrier intent vs the office SAs' tokenCreator bindings — resource bindings are frozen and need no audit loop).

## Verbs and orderings (recast 260612; words open)

Enfeoff/escheat survive with single-binding bodies;
enfranchise/expel survive as admission/removal compositions;
**recut retires** (the IdP owns rotation; there is nothing to rotate Google-side);
**credential delivery retires whole** (nothing is delivered; the assay machinery vacates with it).

| Verb | Body | Crash leaves |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise | terrier write → tokenCreator binding | visible deficit; re-run (idempotent) |
| enfeoff | terrier write → tokenCreator binding on further office | same |
| escheat | terrier withdraw → remove binding | visible surplus; report-only |
| expel | escheat all offices → deregister note (IdP-side removal is the IdP admin's) | partial teardown lands as surplus, never resurrection |
| read-Terrier | pure read | — |

The vocabulary itself is **open**:
the office noun, the impersonation act, login/session words —
re-minted under MCM Word Selection as the follow-on vocabulary pace (see Open).
Until minted, paddock prose uses *office* as a working placeholder, not an elected quoin.

## Retriever differentiation (cinched 260612 — policy, not mechanism)

Same architecture, three knobs:
retriever-office token lifetime at the 12 h ceiling
(activates the canon's deferred D5 capability-set-keyed lifetime — the trigger case);
machine consumers (CI, crucible charges, runtime pulls) ride their **own workload identities**
granted reader directly — the IdP is never in a machine pull chain;
the reserve-key posture (one key on retriever-office, scoped policy exception)
is documented fallback, **not built**.

## Token custody (cinched MVP shape; upgrades named)

MVP: per-session filesystem scratch behind the accessor seam, 0600, ≤ session cap, never a regime.
First upgrade: OS keystores via container credential-helpers
(one election covers podman and Docker; podman's Linux default is already tmpfs).
Anti-exfiltration layer: VPC-SC ingress with IP access levels (workforce-compatible),
deferred with triggers (first multi-operator org customer or first audit requirement).
Device-bound CBA is currently unavailable to federated principals — named revisit trigger.
Evidence: the two beta-repo memos of 2026-06-11 (custody/context-enforcement; impersonation preference).

## Launcher surface (carried forward, one addition)

`rbw-p` polity and `rbw-m` manor families as elected;
`rbw-m` levy gains establish-federation and office establishment;
recut leaves the `rbw-p` surface;
the retirement map otherwise stands.

## Documentation strategy (cinched, carried forward)

Contract specs ride their pace contract-first with in-pace predecessor retirement;
successor acronyms mint at slate-time;
RBS0 civic quoins at the head of the verb movement;
narrative docs trail as their own movement;
post-build cold-probe verifies.

## Ordering — substrate first, spike before verbs (recast 260612)

1. **Accessor seam.** As before: one accessor resolves every credential,
   read-side grep gate, identity-keyed interface, zero behavior change, suites arbiter.
   Keyfile inside during the bridge; the federation branch lands at movement 4.
2. **Capability-sets as named code.** As before — transcription out of invest/mantle bodies;
   these definitions become the office SAs' levy-time grant lists.
3. **Federation spike.** Slated as the heat's first pace; see its docket.
   Gate for movement 4: the legs proven in curl, the banked verification items answered.
4. **Civic verbs + offices + terriers.** The behavior-changing movement:
   RBS0 civic quoins at its head;
   establish-federation + office establishment into levy;
   the three legs in the accessor with session cache and headless fail-fast;
   admission verbs under `rbw-p`; terriers live.
5. **Manor colophon regroup.** As before, plus the new founding gestures.
6. **Narrative docs.** As before.
7. **RBRA estate retirement.** After federation personas pass the suites:
   cult verbs, key machinery, RBSRA and the keyfile probe specs retire whole.
   The bridge's demolition condition, executed.
8. **Handbook rework: home open** (₣A6 vs a final movement here), unchanged.

Movements 1–2 are no-regret and proceed immediately; nothing waits on the spike.

## MVP purpose (carried forward, recast day-after picture)

The realistic pioneer is the solo evaluator — now with domain + org + IdP tenant,
accepted as a one-time founding hour.
Day-after picture:
payor establishes manor + federation trust, levies depot (offices rise with it),
seats self as governor by one binding;
that governor enfeoffs self into director and retriever offices (two bindings);
every tabtarget works through the accessor on a morning device-flow click;
read-Terrier prints three lines;
invest/divest/mantle/roster and every key file no longer exist.

## Test rig (open, the one place a durable secret could creep back)

Synthetic personas need sessions once keys retire.

vs a test-org-only secret (Keycloak password grant or caged token — R4's ghost).
Decide at movement-4 slating, deliberately.

free Entra tenant (real foreign IdP, device flow)
and Keycloak-in-a-crucible (hermetic; JWKS upload makes a private issuer viable for programmatic flow).

One operator may hold several offices but a token carries exactly one office's authority;
cross-office union exists only as serial impersonation, never as one credential.

- Retriever differs by policy only: 12 h lifetime, machine pulls on workload identity, reserve-key posture documented not built.
- Token custody: session scratch behind the accessor at MVP; keystore upgrade and VPC-SC ingress as named deferrals.

toolUseResult.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BZ

## Boundary — single tier, office impersonation (cinched 260612)

This heat builds the **one** operator credential model:
workforce federation with **office-SA impersonation**.
The keyfile citizen tier is **canceled unbuilt** —
no mode enum, no second tier, no migration machinery
(decision record: `Memos/memo-20260612-office-federation-conversion.md`;
canon banner updated same day).
Prerequisite ladder accepted: every manor brings a domain (org anchor, a deed not a building),
a Cloud Identity org, and a conformant external OIDC IdP.
Today's keyfile machinery is **bridge legacy**:
untouched, riding under the accessor seam, suites stay green on it
until federation personas pass the same suites —
then the RBRA estate retires whole as its own movement.
Scaffolding with a demolition condition, never a tier.

## The premise — human present (cinched; earmarked for RBS0)

Unchanged from the canon's D2:
**a human is present at the kickoff of every run; no run outlives a session cap.**
No refresh token anywhere beyond the payor's own;
an orchestrating agent never holds a secret
(device-flow kickoff surfaces only user_code + verification URI).
Belongs in RBS0 as a premise quoin voiced `axk_premise` at civic incorporation.

## The office architecture (cinched 260612)

**Capability-sets instantiate as office SAs at levy; all resource IAM freezes there.**

- Levy creates the office SAs (governor / director / retriever) beside mason
  and grants **every** resource binding (project, GAR repo, mason actAs) to them, once,
  behind a settle gate — levy is long and payor-driven; the gate is cheap there.
- Admission to an office is **one binding**:
  `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` on the office SA, naming the operator's `principal://` subject.
  Removal is that binding's deletion.
- Runtime chain: IdP device flow (Leg 1) → STS exchange (Leg 2) →
  `generateAccessToken` on the office SA (Leg 3) → short-lived office token.
  Everything downstream bearer-blind, unchanged.
- **Zero service account keys exist anywhere**; the depot runs with
  `disableServiceAccountKeyCreation` fully enforced.
  The system's sole durable secret is the payor's RBRO refresh token.
- No per-user Google identity ever exists:
  the federated principal is the grantable identity, implicit in the IdP assertion.
  The IdP is the census (cinch carried forward).
- Flap payoff (the architecture's origin): post-levy IAM mutation collapses to
  one binding type at one scope with one gate point;
  the SA-lifecycle flap family confines to levy;
  hot paths perform zero IAM mutation.
  Office tokens are uncapped and self-expiring; the 10-key cap is irrelevant (no keys).
- An impersonated office token is a plain SA token —
  the workforce federated-token per-product support matrix is dodged entirely.
- Blast radius: a token is one office at a time;
  the multi-hat union-credential concern dissolves.

## The civic structure (cinched, carried forward with office recast)

**Payor founds, governors populate, terriers tell, IAM enforces.**

- Admission authority scoped to the polity it admits into; payor outside the citizenry,
  ceremonial after founding; founding gestures gain **establish-federation**
  (workforce pool + provider + attribute mapping, org-level, once per manor)
  and **office establishment** at levy.
- All operator administration is governor-wielded and depot-scoped.
  A governor admits another to the governor office — governors create governors.
  Cross-depot administration does not exist.
- Governor dissolves structurally: an operator holding the governor office.
  Standing governor remains the default posture.
- One-identity-across-depots arrives free:
  the same `principal://` subject is grantable in every depot under the manor.

## Multi-IdP posture (cinched 260612)

One live provider per pool is the norm.
Subjects are **pool-scoped, not provider-scoped** —
every provider is a full root of trust for the whole subject namespace,
so the pool's security floor is its weakest provider.
Adding a provider is a payor ceremony with founding gravity.
Dual-provider windows exist for IdP **migration** only (overlap, re-admit, retire) —
never as an availability hedge; live tokens already ride out IdP outages within the session cap.

## The Terrier (carried forward; entries recast)

Per-polity terriers in the Manor-homed Terrier bucket, managed-folder grain,
payor-created at levy, governor-writeable own-terrier-only,
read population governors-and-above — all unchanged.
Entries now record (principal subject, office held);
key IDs vanish with the keys.
Working verbs never touch terriers; audit is a read-only diff
(terrier intent vs the office SAs' tokenCreator bindings — resource bindings are frozen and need no audit loop).

## Verbs and orderings (recast 260612; words open)

Enfeoff/escheat survive with single-binding bodies;
enfranchise/expel survive as admission/removal compositions;
**recut retires** (the IdP owns rotation; there is nothing to rotate Google-side);
**credential delivery retires whole** (nothing is delivered; the assay machinery vacates with it).

| Verb | Body | Crash leaves |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise | terrier write → tokenCreator binding | visible deficit; re-run (idempotent) |
| enfeoff | terrier write → tokenCreator binding on further office | same |
| escheat | terrier withdraw → remove binding | visible surplus; report-only |
| expel | escheat all offices → deregister note (IdP-side removal is the IdP admin's) | partial teardown lands as surplus, never resurrection |
| read-Terrier | pure read | — |

The vocabulary itself is **open**:
the office noun, the impersonation act, login/session words —
re-minted under MCM Word Selection as the follow-on vocabulary pace (see Open).
Until minted, paddock prose uses *office* as a working placeholder, not an elected quoin.

## Retriever differentiation (cinched 260612 — policy, not mechanism)

Same architecture, three knobs:
retriever-office token lifetime at the 12 h ceiling
(activates the canon's deferred D5 capability-set-keyed lifetime — the trigger case);
machine consumers (CI, crucible charges, runtime pulls) ride their **own workload identities**
granted reader directly — the IdP is never in a machine pull chain;
the reserve-key posture (one key on retriever-office, scoped policy exception)
is documented fallback, **not built**.

## Token custody (cinched MVP shape; upgrades named)

MVP: per-session filesystem scratch behind the accessor seam, 0600, ≤ session cap, never a regime.
First upgrade: OS keystores via container credential-helpers
(one election covers podman and Docker; podman's Linux default is already tmpfs).
Anti-exfiltration layer: VPC-SC ingress with IP access levels (workforce-compatible),
deferred with triggers (first multi-operator org customer or first audit requirement).
Device-bound CBA is currently unavailable to federated principals — named revisit trigger.
Evidence: the two beta-repo memos of 2026-06-11 (custody/context-enforcement; impersonation preference).

## Launcher surface (carried forward, one addition)

`rbw-p` polity and `rbw-m` manor families as elected;
`rbw-m` levy gains establish-federation and office establishment;
recut leaves the `rbw-p` surface;
the retirement map otherwise stands.

## Documentation strategy (cinched, carried forward)

Contract specs ride their pace contract-first with in-pace predecessor retirement;
successor acronyms mint at slate-time;
RBS0 civic quoins at the head of the verb movement;
narrative docs trail as their own movement;
post-build cold-probe verifies.

## Ordering — substrate first, spike before verbs (recast 260612)

1. **Accessor seam.** As before: one accessor resolves every credential,
   read-side grep gate, identity-keyed interface, zero behavior change, suites arbiter.
   Keyfile inside during the bridge; the federation branch lands at movement 4.
2. **Capability-sets as named code.** As before — transcription out of invest/mantle bodies;
   these definitions become the office SAs' levy-time grant lists.
3. **Federation spike.** Slated as the heat's first pace; see its docket.
   Gate for movement 4: the legs proven in curl, the banked verification items answered.
4. **Civic verbs + offices + terriers.** The behavior-changing movement:
   RBS0 civic quoins at its head;
   establish-federation + office establishment into levy;
   the three legs in the accessor with session cache and headless fail-fast;
   admission verbs under `rbw-p`; terriers live.
5. **Manor colophon regroup.** As before, plus the new founding gestures.
6. **Narrative docs.** As before.
7. **RBRA estate retirement.** After federation personas pass the suites:
   cult verbs, key machinery, RBSRA and the keyfile probe specs retire whole.
   The bridge's demolition condition, executed.
8. **Handbook rework: home open** (₣A6 vs a final movement here), unchanged.

Movements 1–2 are no-regret and proceed immediately; nothing waits on the spike.

## MVP purpose (carried forward, recast day-after picture)

The realistic pioneer is the solo evaluator — now with domain + org + IdP tenant,
accepted as a one-time founding hour.
Day-after picture:
payor establishes manor + federation trust, levies depot (offices rise with it),
seats self as governor by one binding;
that governor enfeoffs self into director and retriever offices (two bindings);
every tabtarget works through the accessor on a morning device-flow click;
read-Terrier prints three lines;
invest/divest/mantle/roster and every key file no longer exist.

input.content:
# jjezs_slate federation-legs-spike

Prove the office-federation runtime chain end-to-end in pure curl/openssl/jq before the verb movement is slated,
and answer the banked verification items.

Stand up on the existing org: a workforce pool, an OIDC provider against a test IdP
(free Entra tenant; also assess Keycloak-in-a-crucible viability via uploaded JWKS for the hermetic case),
and walk: device flow (Leg 1) → STS exchange (Leg 2) → `generateAccessToken` on a test office SA (Leg 3) →
bearer call against a depot API.
No gcloud at runtime; setup ceremony may use console/gcloud freely.
Elect the session-cache wiring shape (token shared across tabtarget processes,
per-session scratch, ≤ session cap, fail-loud when absent and no human can click).

Sources: `Memos/memo-20260612-office-federation-conversion.md` (decision record),
`Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md` (Leg 1/2 mechanics),
beta-repo evidence memos of 2026-06-11 (custody, impersonation preference).

## Done when

- A federated principal mints an office-SA token from a clean shell and successfully calls a depot API with it.
- Session-cache shape elected and recorded (memo or paddock note).
- Verification items answered with evidence: audit-log delegationInfo shape under impersonation;
  office-token lifetime ceiling and the lifetime-extension org-policy constraint;
  Artifact Registry and Cloud Build status in the workforce federated-token support matrix;
  STS egress-rule shape under a VPC-SC perimeter (paper answer acceptable);
  Keycloak-uploaded-JWKS programmatic flow viability (paper answer acceptable if Entra leg consumed the budget).
- Findings land as a memo; surprises that change the paddock's office-architecture section are flagged, not silently absorbed.

## Cinched

- This is evidence-gathering, not production code: throwaway scripts acceptable, BCG discipline not required.
- The pool/provider created here may serve as the project's standing test trust if it proves clean.

## Character

Exploratory spike against foreign APIs; expect Palisade surprises; characterize precisely rather than tolerate silently.

All landed. The rename work I saw in the tree (zipper, generated files) is untouched — your other workstream, as you said.

**Committed:**
- `Memos/memo-20260612-office-federation-conversion.md` — the decision record, supersession map, deletion ledger (4feb09ae)
- Canon banner on memo-20260609 — D1 retired, office impersonation supersedes direct grants, D5's retriever refinement activated (same commit)
- ₣BZ paddock rewritten whole — office architecture, bridge/demolition policy, seven-movement ordering, multi-IdP and retriever cinches, test-rig open (f7443c8e)
- Heat silks: `rbk-14-office-federation` (806c515f)
- **₢BZAAA (federation-legs-spike)** slated as the heat's first pace — three legs in pure curl, Entra + Keycloak-JWKS assessment, session-cache election, the banked verification items as its Done-when

## The vocabulary mull, opened

The mint inventory, as I see it — five slots, in rough order of how much they constrain everything else:

| Slot | Placeholder | Lands in | Seed thoughts |
|---|---|---|---|
| The standing role-SA noun | *office* | SA names, quoins, terrier entries, maybe colophons | *office* may be too trodden (back-office, Office suites). Manorial register has real candidates: **serjeanty** (tenure-by-service — eerily exact), **bailiwick** (sphere of office) |
| The impersonation act | *impersonate* | accessor verb, specs, prose everywhere | *impersonate* is IAM's own vocabulary — same disqualification as grant/revoke. *assume* is AWS's. Fresh mint needed |
| The per-session human act | *login / device flow* | the Leg-1 verb, onboarding prose | **homage** is tempting — the per-session act of proving presence to one's lord is literally what the device-flow click is. Rare, real, feudal-register |
| The founding of IdP trust | *establish-federation* | the payor verb, `rbw-m` colophon | diplomatic/ecclesiastical register: **concordat** (a treaty between realms — manor and foreign IdP) |
| Terrier-entry noun + read verb | — | folds in the old open (*feoffment*, *consult* candidates) | unchanged from prior paddock |

One trap to name before we start: **mantle and invest are retiring, and both would fit the impersonation act beautifully — and neither can be reused.** MCM semantic uniqueness is "one meaning per word, *ever*"; re-minting a retired cult verb with a new meaning is word-cancer with your own history. The retired words are spent.

And one genuine question for you before candidates harden: does **citizen** survive? The tier it named is dead, but the civic structure (citizenry, enfranchise, polity) is woven through the paddock and reads naturally for IdP-scoped operators. My instinct: keep it — the asterism holds and the word's meaning ("admitted member of a polity") didn't change, only the admission mechanism did. But it's your register to rule on.

Where do you want to start — the office noun, since it constrains SA names and the spike will want to mint test SAs?

message.content: I will expressly weaken the requirement to retire names; we are pre MVP so we can reuse words.  But indeed it takes some caution to fully evict prior terms to prevent cancer from confusion.  By the way, can I run the spike in a parallel repo safely?  Is that pace well enough slated and standalone ?

content.text:
Ruling noted, and it's the right call pre-MVP: reuse is allowed, **eviction precedes re-mint** — which the existing grep gate already enforces mechanically, since a reused word can't grep clean until every prior-meaning site is swept. Mantle and invest return to the candidate pool carrying that tax. I'll carry this into the vocabulary pace's docket when we slate it.

**Parallel repo: yes, safely — with three conditions.**

1. **Push first.** The conversion memo, canon banner, paddock, and ₢BZAAA exist only in this clone until pushed; a parallel clone needs the pull to even see the pace.
2. **One clone owns the pace's state.** Gallops state syncs via git like any file — two clones notching/wrapping the same pace produces merge conflicts in the enrollment ledger. Do the spike's `jjx_record`/wrap from whichever clone runs it, and let this one stay read-only for ₣BZ until it syncs back.
3. **Beta-repo references are sibling-relative.** The docket cites the beta evidence memos by name, not path, so they're findable — but if the parallel clone doesn't sit beside `rbm_beta_recipemuster`, that's a lookup, not a link.

**Standalone-ness of the docket: yes, deliberately.** Its work is almost entirely cloud-side — org-level pool/provider creation (additive, touches no existing depot or SA), one test office SA, throwaway curl scripts, findings as a memo. It names its sources by repo path, carries its Done-when in full, pins nothing to line numbers or working-tree paths, and leaves project/depot selection to mount time. The cloud state it mutates is orthogonal to anything the suites or your rename workstream touch, so it can run while this clone churns through the source-file renames without contention — which I suspect is exactly why you're asking.

One small honesty note: the spike *will* want a depot API to bearer-call at the end (the canonical standing depot is the obvious target, read-only call) — if the rename workstream plans to unmake or re-levy depots in the same window, sequence that.

content.content:
905	== Word Selection
906	
907	Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
908	this section governs the right half — the word itself.
909	The rules below bind the word part of every
910	{mcm_quoin},
911	the display text of every
912	{mcm_linked_term},
913	and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
914	They constrain births, not the living —
915	existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.
916	
917	* *Semantic uniqueness.*
918	One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
919	A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
920	* *Vocabulary isolation.*
921	Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
922	A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
923	{mcm_trodden_word}
924	and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
925	Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
926	word-level checks merely verify.
927	* *The grep gate.*
928	A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
929	a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
930	* *Rare but real.*
931	Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
932	Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
933	Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
934	* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
935	A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
936	a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
937	* *Audible register.*
938	Sibling subsystems ride
939	{mcm_asterism_s}
940	whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
941	so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.
942	
943	[[mcm_asterism]]
944	{mcm_asterism}::
945	A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
946	The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
947	as a vocabulary family may span
948	{mcm_concept_model_s}.
949	Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
950	(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
951	so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
952	and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
953	for the
954	{mcm_digital_mind}
955	and the
956	{mcm_editor}
957	alike.
958	Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
959	Model-side rationale: `Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
960	prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.
961	
962	== Antipatterns
963	
964	[[mcm_antipattern]]
965	{mcm_antipattern}::
966	A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
967	{mcm_curation_action_s}
968	and reviews can cite it precisely.
969	Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
970	an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.
971	
972	[[mcm_tidemark]]
973	{mcm_tidemark}::
974	An
975	{mcm_antipattern}:
976	version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
977	The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
978	History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
979	git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
980	{mcm_tidemark_s}
981	drift and pollute present-tense reading.
982	A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
983	A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
984	Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).
985	
986	[[mcm_trodden_word]]
987	{mcm_trodden_word}::
988	An
989	{mcm_antipattern}:
990	a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —
991	open, close, run, lock, task, manager, handler, grant.
992	The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
993	grep returns a thousand strangers.
994	A
995	{mcm_trodden_word}
996	fails retrieval for the
997	{mcm_digital_mind}
998	and search for the
999	{mcm_editor},
1000	and invites the plan-versus-product bleed that vocabulary isolation exists to prevent.
1001	Semantic fit never rescues it:
1002	the word is disqualified before fit is weighed.
1003	
1004	== Document Elements

file.content:
== Word Selection

Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
this section governs the right half — the word itself.
The rules below bind the word part of every
{mcm_quoin},
the display text of every
{mcm_linked_term},
and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
They constrain births, not the living —
existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.

* *Semantic uniqueness.*
One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
* *Vocabulary isolation.*
Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
{mcm_trodden_word}
and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
word-level checks merely verify.
* *The grep gate.*
A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
* *Rare but real.*
Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
* *Audible register.*
Sibling subsystems ride
{mcm_asterism_s}
whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.

[[mcm_asterism]]
{mcm_asterism}::
A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
as a vocabulary family may span
{mcm_concept_model_s}.
Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and the
{mcm_editor}
alike.
Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
Model-side rationale: `Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.

== Antipatterns

[[mcm_antipattern]]
{mcm_antipattern}::
A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
{mcm_curation_action_s}
and reviews can cite it precisely.
Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.

[[mcm_tidemark]]
{mcm_tidemark}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
{mcm_tidemark_s}
drift and pollute present-tense reading.
A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).

[[mcm_trodden_word]]
{mcm_trodden_word}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —
open, close, run, lock, task, manager, handler, grant.
The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
grep returns a thousand strangers.
A
{mcm_trodden_word}
fails retrieval for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and search for the
{mcm_editor},
and invites the plan-versus-product bleed that vocabulary isolation exists to prevent.
Semantic fit never rescues it:
the word is disqualified before fit is weighed.

== Document Elements

content.text:
Found it — MCM gained **Word Selection** (the word-part complement to prefix discipline): six binding rules — semantic uniqueness repo-wide, vocabulary isolation from software-adjacent domains, the grep gate, rare-but-real, mint into an asterism, audible register — plus two new quoins, `mcm_asterism` (named metaphor family doing structural work) and `mcm_trodden_word` (disqualified before semantic fit is weighed). And notably: "constrains births, not the living" — which your eviction-then-reuse ruling extends cleanly.

Here's the context assembly, and I'll hold off on candidate words — I think the jangle came from my proposing exotica before the frame was set.

## The discipline your reframe adds to MCM

MCM's rules serve the digital mind and the editor — retrieval, grep, register. User-facing names carry a constraint MCM doesn't state: **comprehension at first contact** — an evaluator must act correctly on first reading, mid-ceremony, without a glossary lookup. Your existing public register already shows the resolution: *Bottle, Depot, Levy, Summon* — rare-but-real AND concrete enough to half-guess. My *serjeanty/concordat* class aces MCM and fails this test. The target is the intersection.

## The user-facing inventory under office-federation, by traffic

| Traffic | Slot | Today's word(s) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Daily** | the sign-in act (device-flow click) | — (new) | **Top priority mint.** Highest-frequency user verb in the whole system, and its trodden defaults (*login, session, auth*) are maximally disqualified |
| **Daily** | the live authenticated window + its expiry | — (new) | Same — users must reason about "why is it asking again" |
| Occasional | admission lifecycle | Mantle, Invest, Divest, Roster (all retire) | Mint set — but see shape question (a) |
| Once | founding of IdP trust | — (new) | Joins the Establish/Levy founding family |
| Nouns | the three roles | **Governor, Director, Retriever — survive as the office names** | No mint needed; users say "I'm the director." The *generic* office noun is mostly spec-internal — lower priority than I'd been treating it |
| Nouns | the rights ledger | Terrier | Already minted |
| Nouns | the operator identity | citizen? | Pending your ruling |
| Vanishing | keys, RBRA placement, probes-as-users-know-them | Install, Rekey, JWTProbe | Users stop needing these words at all |

## Register observation

Your asterisms already separate by ear, which the new MCM rule now codifies: credential/polity rides **manorial-civic** (Manor, Levy, Payor, Governor, Terrier), foundry rides **ecclesiastical** (Ordain, Conjure, Vouch, Abjure), crucible rides **alchemical** (Charge, Quench, Scry). New credential mints should join the first and stay audible against the other two.

## Shape questions to rule on before any words

(a) **How many admission verbs do users face?** The paddock's four (enfranchise/expel/enfeoff/escheat) were chosen for internal crash-ordering precision. The user surface might want two — *admit to an office / remove from an office* — with the four-way precision living in specs only.
(b) **Does *citizen* survive** as the operator noun, now that identity is IdP-scoped?
(c) **One sign-in word for all humans including the payor**, or does the payor's OAuth ceremony keep separate wording?
(d) Does the generic office noun appear in user prose at all, or only in specs?

Rule on these and the candidate conversation has rails.

aiTitle: Discuss federation vs citizen model architecture

message.content: Stop trying to pick the names now, I think I stated my intent this choice belongs in a pace after the spike.  Did I say that?  I want this chat to be about further codifying my naming practice so that we can refer to it and test it when we mount that pace.

content.text:
You said the *mull* comes next, and the choice rides a pace — I supplied candidates twice anyway. Dropped; this conversation is doctrine only.

Here's a draft codification, shaped so the future pace can cite it and test against it. Strawman for you to carve:

## Exposure tiers (the new axis your practice already uses)

Every persistent name gets classified **before** minting:

- **T1 — Operator vocabulary.** Words a user triggers, observes in output/errors, or needs to reason about state. The README glossary anchor set *is* this tier's registry — presence in the glossary is the contract.
- **T2 — Spec-controlled.** Quoins and module words under RBS0/MCM control. Full Word Selection rules; no learnability burden.
- **T3 — Interior.** Implementation names behind prefix discipline; word choice unceremonied.

Your stated practice, made explicit: discipline concentrates at T1, RBS0 holds T2, and T3 is deliberately let go.

## T1's additional binding rules (beyond MCM's six)

1. **First-contact comprehension.** An operator mid-ceremony must act correctly on first encounter, no glossary lookup. Concrete, physical, half-self-teaching (the *Bottle/Levy* standard). This deliberately *tightens* rare-but-real: rare enough to grep clean, common enough to guess warm.
2. **Traffic-weighted scrutiny.** Care proportional to contact frequency. A daily word outranks a founding-ceremony word; both outrank a spec word.
3. **User-perspective verbs.** A T1 verb names what the operator does or sees, never the mechanism underneath ("admit someone," not the binding that implements it).
4. **Noun earns its sentences.** A T1 noun is justified by the state-reasoning sentences it enables; if users never need to say it, it isn't T1 — push it down a tier.
5. **Vocabulary budget.** The T1 set is bounded; internal precision collapses into fewer user words wherever the distinction doesn't change what the user does. (Spec keeps the fine grain.)
6. **Error surfaces are T1.** Words appearing in failure output are operator vocabulary even if the operator never invokes them.

## Testable gates for the pace

- Grep gate (existing, unchanged).
- **Classification ledger**: each mint records its tier and, for T1, its traffic class — the pace's deliverable includes this table.
- **Cold-contact probe**: a fresh reader (agent or human) gets one sentence per T1 candidate in context and states what they'd expect it to do — your existing cold-probe pattern, applied to words.
- **Glossary registration**: T1 mint isn't done until the README anchor exists.
- Register check by ear against the sibling asterisms (MCM's audible-register rule, applied at T1 where it matters most).

Open carving questions: (1) is three tiers right, or does "user must know about" vs "user triggers" split T1? (2) Does this live as an MCM subsection under Word Selection, a CLAUDE.md echo, or a standalone doctrine doc the pace cites? (3) Does the budget rule deserve a number, or stay qualitative?

content: Converting Recipe Bottle to single-tier federation with office-SA impersonation: paddock rewritten, decision memo committed, spike pace ₢BZAAA slated. Now drafting naming-practice doctrine (exposure tiers, T1 rules, testable gates); next you carve the three open questions. (disable recaps in /config)

383	== Core Principles
384	
385	The Axial Lexicon provides reusable, atomic terminology for technical specifications.
386	Each term maintains precise semantics while adapting to implementation context.
387	Specifications selectively import only needed axial terms, avoiding vocabulary bloat.
388	
389	== Motif Vocabulary
390	
391	AXL terms are
392	{axl_motif_s}
393	- abstract patterns that concrete specifications voice in their local context.
394	
395	[[axl_motif]]
396	{axl_motif}::
397	An abstract pattern defined in the Axial Lexicon that can be voiced by concrete terms in specifications.
398	A
399	{axl_motif}
400	captures the essential shape of a concept while remaining independent of implementation context.
401	Specifications voice
402	{axl_motif_s}
403	through local terms that bind the abstract pattern to specific environments.
404	
405	[[axl_voicing]]
406	{axl_voicing}::
407	A concrete term in a specification that gives local expression to A
408	{axl_motif}.
409	Each
410	{axl_voicing}
411	adapts the abstract pattern to a specific context while preserving the
412	{axl_motif_s}
413	essential character.
414	
415	[[axl_voices]]
416	{axl_voices}::
417	The relationship where a concrete term expresses a
418	{axl_motif}
419	in a specific context.
420	When a term
421	{axl_voices}
422	a
423	{axl_motif},
424	it gives the abstract pattern local form and implementation-specific behavior.
425	
426	[[axl_evokes]]
427	{axl_evokes}::
428	The relationship where A
429	{axl_motif}
430	calls forth its
431	{axl_voicing_s}
432	in the reader's understanding.
433	When encountering A
434	{axl_motif},
435	it
436	{axl_evokes}
437	the concrete realizations known from various specifications.
438	
439	[[axl_annotation]]
440	{axl_annotation}::
441	A
442	{xref_MCM}
443	comment line that declares the
444	{axl_motif_s}
445	a term
446	{axl_voices}.
447	The
448	{axl_annotation}
449	appears between anchor and definition as a space-separated list of prefix terms on a comment line with no space after `//`.
450	+
451	Format: `//relationship primary [dimension ...]`
452	+
453	* First term: the relationship (typically `axl_voices`)
454	* Second term: the primary
455	{axl_motif}
456	being voiced
457	* Remaining terms: dimension terms (prefixed `axd_`) specifying characteristics
458	+
459	Examples:
460	+
461	* `//axl_voices axc_call` - simple voicing
462	* `//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable` - voicing with dimensions
463	+
464	Category prefixes carry semantic meaning, eliminating need for punctuation.
465	Position determines role: relationship first, primary second, dimensions following.
466	This design prioritizes LLM interpretability over human visual parsing.
467	When ambiguity or friction arises during LLM processing, the format should evolve to resolve it.
468	
469	== Motif Implications
470	
471	{axl_motif_s}
472	carry implied characteristics beyond their definitions.
473	These implications guide how
474	{axl_voicing_s}
475	should behave:
476	
477	* Anchor placement expectations per
478	{xref_MCM}
479	form patterns
480	* Type/instance level - where the
481	{axl_motif}
482	sits in abstraction hierarchy
483	* Contextual requirements - parent structures or operations the
484	{axl_motif}
485	exists within
486	* Voicing expectation - whether the
487	{axl_motif}
488	typically requires local voicing
489	
490	== Form Expectations
491	
492	Each AXL category implies an expected
493	{xref_MCM}
494	definition form pattern for documents using that category.
495	During type-scheme linting, these expectations guide validation of definition structure.
496	
497	* {axc_*} control terms expect
498	{xref_MCM}
499	`mcm_form_deflist`
500	* {axj_structure} definitions expect
501	{xref_MCM}
502	`mcm_form_section`
503	* {axj_field} definitions expect
504	{xref_MCM}
505	`mcm_form_bullet` under parent structure
506	* {axk_premise} definitions expect
507	{xref_MCM}
508	`mcm_form_deflist`
509	* Most other categories expect
510	{xref_MCM}
511	`mcm_form_deflist`
512	
513	These expectations support the
514	{axl_annotation}
515	pattern where documents include `//axl_voices motif ...` markers that are validated when AXL is in context.
516	
517	== Axial Control Terms

aiTitle: Discuss federation vs citizen model architecture

content.text:
Read AXLA. Verdict: **the doctrine lives in MCM; AXLA gets the classification machinery.** And the split is more elegant than a compromise — AXLA's annotation channel is exactly the enforcement wiring the doctrine needs.

## The placement logic

MCM governs *births* — Word Selection is already its jurisdiction, and the exposure-tier doctrine is a birth rule-set. AXLA holds *voiceable abstractions* that concrete specs annotate at definition sites (`//axl_voices motif axd_dimension...`). So:

- **MCM**: the deepened Word Selection section — tiers, the T1 rules, the gates — with quoins for the load-bearing concepts.
- **AXLA**: exposure-tier **dimension terms** (the `axd_` category already exists for exactly this — `axd_required`, `axd_nullable`). A quoin's definition site then *declares its tier* in its annotation, which makes your classification ledger greppable rather than a side table, and makes the gates lintable through machinery that already exists: a T1-annotated quoin missing its registry anchor is a detectable defect, not a review opinion.

That last point is what AXLA buys you: the doctrine stops being prose-you-must-remember and becomes annotations-the-linter-reads.

## Which concepts earn quoins (load-bearing test applied)

Minting actual quoins is right — these will be *cited*: paddocks, dockets, and reviews need to say "rejected under ⟨first-contact rule⟩" addressably. But each must earn it:

| Concept | Quoin? | Why |
|---|---|---|
| The exposure-tier axis itself | yes | the classification every mint now passes through |
| The operator-vocabulary tier (T1) | yes | the most-cited term — "this is T1, the rules bind" |
| First-contact comprehension | yes | the rejection rule reviews will invoke by name |
| Vocabulary budget | yes | cited whenever internal precision collapses into fewer user words |
| Traffic weighting | probably prose | a property of T1 scrutiny, not independently addressable |
| T2/T3 | prose | the unmarked cases; quoining them is non-load-bearing symmetry |
| Cold-contact probe | **curation action, not quoin** | MCM already has `mcm_curation_action_s` — the probe is a *move*, and that's its native register |
| Registry surface | yes, but stated generically | MCM says "each project designates one"; RB's happens to be the README glossary |

Per your instruction I'm not proposing the quoin *words* — they're T2 mints chosen at write time, under the section's own rules. The recursion is the point: the section that codifies word selection must pass its own gates, and that's a feature worth letting the document demonstrate.

## On primacy

Your phrase — "their primacy, in software and systems that I choreograph" — deserves to be the section's opening stance, not buried in rules: something asserting that the concept-model vocabulary *is* the system's primary interface, that names are how work survives across minds (biological and digital), and that the software-specification machinery around it is secondary scaffolding. MCM currently states rules; it doesn't yet state *why the rules outrank convenience*. A short doctrinal preamble — possibly itself a quoin, since paddocks will want to cite the principle — would carry that.

Two questions before drafting: (1) MCM edits have been riding heat ₣Bd — does this amendment land there, or is it part of ₣BZ's vocabulary preparation? (2) Want the draft in-chat first for carving, or straight into the documents with you reviewing the diff?

93	// end::mapping-section[]
94	
95	== Core Structure
96	
97	A
98	{mcm_concept_model}
99	creates a cohesive gestalt - a unified constellation of ideas whose relationships and interactions transcend their individual definitions.
100	Through carefully curated terminology and precise linking patterns, each
101	{mcm_concept_model}
102	illuminates the deep conceptual relationships that give meaning to a domain.
103	
104	The power of a
105	{mcm_concept_model}
106	lies in its ability to:
107	
108	* Reveal the natural connections between ideas
109	* Preserve the integrity of concept relationships through evolution
110	* Enable clear visualization of how changes to one concept ripple through the constellation
111	* Guide understanding through carefully mapped idea spaces
112	
113	Each
114	{mcm_concept_model}
115	manifests through a
116	{mcm_document}
117	that follows strict organizational patterns.
118	These patterns ensure that as the constellation evolves, their relationships remain clear and traceable.
119	
120	The
121	{mcm_document}
122	contains two primary constrained element types:
123	
124	* A
125	{mcm_mapping_section}
126	that defines all
127	{mcm_linked_term_s}
128	* One or more
129	{mcm_definition_section_s}
130	that provide precise meaning for each term
131	
132	Through these elements, the
133	{mcm_document}
134	maintains the delicate balance between rigorous structure and organic concept evolution, allowing the idea constellation to grow while preserving its essential relationships.
135	
136	== Text Organization
137	
138	Text within a
139	{mcm_document}
140	follows specific structural rules to enable clear tracking of changes and relationships.
141	
142	=== Line Break Fundamentals
143	
144	The most fundamental organizing principle is the
145	{mcm_line_break}
146	.
147	Each
148	{mcm_line_break}
149	creates a clear boundary in the
150	{mcm_document}
151	while preserving fluid rendering in the output document.
152	
153	==== Basic Line Break Rules
154	
155	{mcm_line_break_s}
156	occur in these precise patterns:
157	
158	* Between independent sentences
159	* Before and after each
160	{mcm_linked_term}
161	(including each term within a sequence)
162	* Between fragments that surround
163	{mcm_linked_term_s}
164	
165	Basic example showing sentence and term breaks:
166	
167	[source,asciidoc]
168	----
169	The system initializes its components.
170	The
171	{excm_processor}
172	begins operation.
173	Each component reports its status.
174	----
175	
176	==== Term Integration Patterns
177	
178	When incorporating
179	{mcm_linked_term_s}
180	the text follows these specific patterns:
181	
182	Single term integration - breaks before and after the term:
183	
184	[source,asciidoc]
185	----
186	The system processes
187	{excm_request}
188	according to defined rules.
189	----
190	
191	Multiple
192	{mcm_linked_term_s}
193	in sequence - each term gets its own line, with commas attached:
194	
195	[source,asciidoc]
196	----
197	The interface supports
198	{excm_drag},
199	{excm_drop},
200	and
201	{excm_scroll}
202	operations.
203	----
204	
205	Complex
206	{mcm_linked_term}
207	relationships - each standalone term gets breaks:
208	
209	[source,asciidoc]
210	----
211	The
212	{excm_validator}
213	applies each
214	{excm_rule}
215	to ensure
216	{excm_compliance}
217	with system requirements.
218	----
219	
220	These patterns apply consistently across all document contexts, including:
221	
222	* Regular paragraphs
223	* List items
224	* Definition entries
225	* Section introductions
226	
227	=== Paragraph Organization
228	
229	Paragraphs in the
230	{mcm_document}
231	follow these structural principles:
232	
233	* {mcm_blank_line_s}
234	appear only between true paragraphs
235	* No
236	{mcm_blank_line_s}
237	between sentences within a paragraph
238	* No
239	{mcm_blank_line_s}
240	around source code blocks or list items
241	* Each paragraph focuses on a single concept or relationship
242	
243	Example paragraph structure:
244	
245	[source,asciidoc]
246	----
247	The system initializes its core components.
248	Each component performs self-diagnostics.
249	The
250	{excm_validator}
251	confirms system health.
252	
253	The initialization process then moves to external systems.
254	The
255	{excm_connector}
256	establishes required links.
257	Each connection undergoes verification.
258	----
259	
260	=== Special Cases
261	
262	==== List Items
263	
264	The
265	{mcm_document}
266	presents list items
267	with proper term integration
268	and conceptual breaks:
269	
270	[source,asciidoc]
271	----
272	* The first item spans
273	{excm_component}
274	boundaries
275	* A second item demonstrates
276	{excm_process}
277	flow
278	* The final item shows
279	{excm_result}
280	formatting
281	----
282	
283	==== Definition Entries
284	
285	Definition entries follow standard sentence breaks while maintaining definition list structure:
286	
287	The single space following `::` is necessary.
288	
289	[source,asciidoc]
290	----
291	[[excm_term]]
292	{excm_example}:: 
293	The primary definition establishes core meaning.
294	Additional sentences expand understanding.
295	The
296	{excm_linked_term}
297	shows relationship handling.
298	----
299	
300	==== Source Code Boundaries
301	
302	Source code blocks establish strict boundaries for
303	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
304	These boundaries protect code content while maintaining proper term integration in surrounding text:
305	
306	[source,asciidoc]
307	----
308	The
309	{excm_processor}
310	implements this interface:
311	
312	[source,java]
313	-----
314	public interface Processor {
315	    void process();
316	    Status check();
317	}
318	-----
319	
320	The implementation ensures
321	{excm_compliance}
322	with system requirements.
323	----
324	
325	{mcm_curation_action_s}
326	respect these boundaries by:
327	
328	* Maintaining term integration and line breaks in surrounding text
329	* Treating code block contents as opaque
330	* Preserving code formatting without modification
331	* Excluding code content from term matching and promotion analysis
332	
333	This boundary pattern ensures code examples remain stable through document evolution while maintaining proper conceptual integration in their context.
334	
335	=== Punctuation Rules
336	
337	Punctuation follows standard English conventions regardless of line breaks:
338	
339	* Sentence-ending punctuation stays with the sentence
340	* Commas remain with their preceding phrase
341	* Quotation marks enclose their content without breaks
342	* Parentheses maintain content unity
343	
344	Example punctuation handling:
345	
346	[source,asciidoc]
347	----
348	The system (version 2.0) activates its components.
349	The
350	{excm_processor}
351	begins its "primary sequence."
352	After initialization,
353	the system reports "ready."
354	----
355	
356	=== Document Mechanics
357	
358	=== {mcm_mapping_section} Constraints
359	
360	The
361	{mcm_mapping_section}
362	enforces alignment rules to enhance readability and change tracking:
363	
364	* The `<<` of each
365	{mcm_domain}
366	aligns to columns that are multiples of 10
367	* When entries require wider alignment, all lines adjust to the next larger multiple
368	* {mcm_attribute_reference_s}
369	maintain consistent indentation
370	
371	[source,asciidoc]
372	----
373	// tag::mapping-example[]
374	:excm_doc_short:              <<excm_short,Short Term>>
375	:excm_doc_longer_term:        <<excm_longer,Longer Term>>
376	:excm_doc_much_longer_term:   <<excm_much_longer,Much Longer Term>>
377	// end::mapping-example[]
378	----
379	
380	{mcm_normalization}
381	maintains these alignments during curation unless expansion is required.
382	
383	=== Document Source Management
384	
385	The
386	{mcm_document}
387	maintains compatibility with line-based tools
388	while preserving semantic structure.
389	
390	This means:
391	
392	* Favoring AsciiDoc's native list markers
393	over hardcoded sequence numbers
394	
395	* Preserving processor directives
396	that enable clean rendering
397	
398	* Ensuring changes remain visible
399	in both source differencing tools
400	and rendered comparisons
401	
402	The goal is maintaining a document that:
403	
404	* Shows ancestry clearly in source
405	* Renders cleanly through AsciiDoc
406	* Displays changes meaningfully in diffs
407	* Supports post-rendering analysis
408	
409	=== Term Hierarchy Management
410	
411	When
412	{mcm_linked_term_s}
413	appear to form hierarchical relationships, the
414	{mcm_document}
415	flattens this hierarchy through specific patterns:
416	
417	* Each
418	{mcm_linked_term_s}
419	receives first-class presentation in the
420	{mcm_mapping_section}
421	* Related
422	{mcm_linked_term_s}
423	maintain separate definitions
424	* Relationships express through careful definition crafting
425	
426	Example of hierarchical
427	{mcm_linked_term_s}
428	handling:
429	
430	[source,asciidoc]
431	----
432	:excm_doc_validation:             <<excm_validation,Validation>>
433	:excm_doc_validation_rule:        <<excm_validation_rule,Validation Rule>>
434	:excm_doc_validation_set:         <<excm_validation_set,Validation Set>>
435	----
436	
437	The definitions then express relationships:
438	
439	[source,asciidoc]
440	----
441	[[excm_validation]]
442	{excm_doc_validation}:: 
443	The process of applying rules to ensure content correctness.
444	
445	[[excm_validation_rule]]
446	{excm_doc_validation_rule}:: 
447	A specific constraint within the {excm_doc_validation} framework.
448	
449	[[excm_validation_set]]
450	{excm_doc_validation_set}:: 
451	A collection of {excm_doc_validation_rule_s} that work together.
452	----
453	
454	== Definition Patterns
455	
456	=== Definition Form Patterns
457	
458	Each
459	{mcm_linked_term}
460	in a

A
{mcm_concept_model}
creates a cohesive gestalt - a unified constellation of ideas whose relationships and interactions transcend their individual definitions.
Through carefully curated terminology and precise linking patterns, each
{mcm_concept_model}
illuminates the deep conceptual relationships that give meaning to a domain.

The power of a
{mcm_concept_model}
lies in its ability to:

* Reveal the natural connections between ideas
* Preserve the integrity of concept relationships through evolution
* Enable clear visualization of how changes to one concept ripple through the constellation
* Guide understanding through carefully mapped idea spaces

Each
{mcm_concept_model}
manifests through a
{mcm_document}
that follows strict organizational patterns.
These patterns ensure that as the constellation evolves, their relationships remain clear and traceable.

The
{mcm_document}
contains two primary constrained element types:

* A
{mcm_mapping_section}
that defines all
{mcm_linked_term_s}
* One or more
{mcm_definition_section_s}
that provide precise meaning for each term

Through these elements, the
{mcm_document}
maintains the delicate balance between rigorous structure and organic concept evolution, allowing the idea constellation to grow while preserving its essential relationships.

== Text Organization

Text within a
{mcm_document}
follows specific structural rules to enable clear tracking of changes and relationships.

=== Line Break Fundamentals

The most fundamental organizing principle is the
{mcm_line_break}
.
Each
{mcm_line_break}
creates a clear boundary in the
{mcm_document}
while preserving fluid rendering in the output document.

==== Basic Line Break Rules

{mcm_line_break_s}
occur in these precise patterns:

* Between independent sentences
* Before and after each
{mcm_linked_term}
(including each term within a sequence)
* Between fragments that surround
{mcm_linked_term_s}

Basic example showing sentence and term breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system initializes its components.
The
{excm_processor}
begins operation.
Each component reports its status.
----

==== Term Integration Patterns

When incorporating
{mcm_linked_term_s}
the text follows these specific patterns:

Single term integration - breaks before and after the term:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system processes
{excm_request}
according to defined rules.
----

Multiple
{mcm_linked_term_s}
in sequence - each term gets its own line, with commas attached:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The interface supports
{excm_drag},
{excm_drop},
and
{excm_scroll}
operations.
----

Complex
{mcm_linked_term}
relationships - each standalone term gets breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The
{excm_validator}
applies each
{excm_rule}
to ensure
{excm_compliance}
with system requirements.
----

These patterns apply consistently across all document contexts, including:

* Regular paragraphs
* List items
* Definition entries
* Section introductions

=== Paragraph Organization

Paragraphs in the
{mcm_document}
follow these structural principles:

* {mcm_blank_line_s}
appear only between true paragraphs
* No
{mcm_blank_line_s}
between sentences within a paragraph
* No
{mcm_blank_line_s}
around source code blocks or list items
* Each paragraph focuses on a single concept or relationship

Example paragraph structure:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system initializes its core components.
Each component performs self-diagnostics.
The
{excm_validator}
confirms system health.

The initialization process then moves to external systems.
The
{excm_connector}
establishes required links.
Each connection undergoes verification.
----

=== Special Cases

==== List Items

The
{mcm_document}
presents list items
with proper term integration
and conceptual breaks:

[source,asciidoc]
----
* The first item spans
{excm_component}
boundaries
* A second item demonstrates
{excm_process}
flow
* The final item shows
{excm_result}
formatting
----

==== Definition Entries

Definition entries follow standard sentence breaks while maintaining definition list structure:

The single space following `::` is necessary.

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[excm_term]]
{excm_example}:: 
The primary definition establishes core meaning.
Additional sentences expand understanding.
The
{excm_linked_term}
shows relationship handling.
----

==== Source Code Boundaries

Source code blocks establish strict boundaries for
{mcm_curation_action_s}.
These boundaries protect code content while maintaining proper term integration in surrounding text:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The
{excm_processor}
implements this interface:

[source,java]
-----
public interface Processor {
    void process();
    Status check();
}
-----

The implementation ensures
{excm_compliance}
with system requirements.
----

{mcm_curation_action_s}
respect these boundaries by:

* Maintaining term integration and line breaks in surrounding text
* Treating code block contents as opaque
* Preserving code formatting without modification
* Excluding code content from term matching and promotion analysis

This boundary pattern ensures code examples remain stable through document evolution while maintaining proper conceptual integration in their context.

=== Punctuation Rules

Punctuation follows standard English conventions regardless of line breaks:

* Sentence-ending punctuation stays with the sentence
* Commas remain with their preceding phrase
* Quotation marks enclose their content without breaks
* Parentheses maintain content unity

Example punctuation handling:

[source,asciidoc]
----
The system (version 2.0) activates its components.
The
{excm_processor}
begins its "primary sequence."
After initialization,
the system reports "ready."
----

=== Document Mechanics

=== {mcm_mapping_section} Constraints

The
{mcm_mapping_section}
enforces alignment rules to enhance readability and change tracking:

* The `<<` of each
{mcm_domain}
aligns to columns that are multiples of 10
* When entries require wider alignment, all lines adjust to the next larger multiple
* {mcm_attribute_reference_s}
maintain consistent indentation

[source,asciidoc]
----
// tag::mapping-example[]
:excm_doc_short:              <<excm_short,Short Term>>
:excm_doc_longer_term:        <<excm_longer,Longer Term>>
:excm_doc_much_longer_term:   <<excm_much_longer,Much Longer Term>>
// end::mapping-example[]
----

{mcm_normalization}
maintains these alignments during curation unless expansion is required.

=== Document Source Management

The
{mcm_document}
maintains compatibility with line-based tools
while preserving semantic structure.

This means:

* Favoring AsciiDoc's native list markers
over hardcoded sequence numbers

* Preserving processor directives
that enable clean rendering

* Ensuring changes remain visible
in both source differencing tools
and rendered comparisons

The goal is maintaining a document that:

* Shows ancestry clearly in source
* Renders cleanly through AsciiDoc
* Displays changes meaningfully in diffs
* Supports post-rendering analysis

=== Term Hierarchy Management

When
{mcm_linked_term_s}
appear to form hierarchical relationships, the
{mcm_document}
flattens this hierarchy through specific patterns:

* Each
{mcm_linked_term_s}
receives first-class presentation in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
* Related
{mcm_linked_term_s}
maintain separate definitions
* Relationships express through careful definition crafting

Example of hierarchical
{mcm_linked_term_s}
handling:

[source,asciidoc]
----
:excm_doc_validation:             <<excm_validation,Validation>>
:excm_doc_validation_rule:        <<excm_validation_rule,Validation Rule>>
:excm_doc_validation_set:         <<excm_validation_set,Validation Set>>
----

The definitions then express relationships:

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[excm_validation]]
{excm_doc_validation}:: 
The process of applying rules to ensure content correctness.

[[excm_validation_rule]]
{excm_doc_validation_rule}:: 
A specific constraint within the {excm_doc_validation} framework.

[[excm_validation_set]]
{excm_doc_validation_set}:: 
A collection of {excm_doc_validation_rule_s} that work together.
----

== Definition Patterns

=== Definition Form Patterns

Each
{mcm_linked_term}
in a

content.content:
460	in a
461	{mcm_document}
462	requires an anchor target and a definition.
463	The form determines how these elements are structured.
464	
465	[[mcm_annotation]]
466	{mcm_annotation}::
467	A comment line providing type categorization or metadata about terms in a
468	{mcm_document}.
469	{mcm_annotation_s}
470	are ignored during first-order document reading.
471	When a referencing system is in context,
472	{mcm_annotation_s}
473	provide structured metadata interpreted by that system.
474	The content contains space-separated
475	{mcm_linked_term_s}
476	from the referencing system, which is typically out-of-context during first-order reading.
477	The grammar, interpretation, and placement rules for
478	{mcm_annotation_s}
479	are defined by the referencing system, not MCM.
480	+
481	{mcm_annotation_s}
482	use prefix-discriminated form: `//ax...` — no space after `//`, where the initial characters match a registered prefix identifying the referencing system.
483	The full content after `//` is interpreted by the referencing system as space-separated prefix terms.
484	+
485	The mechanical discriminator is the character immediately following `//`.
486	A space character indicates a regular comment.
487	A letter with no intervening space indicates an
488	{mcm_annotation}.
489	+
490	Registered prefixes:
491	+
492	* `ax` — Axial Lexicon
493	
494	[[mcm_form_deflist]]
495	{mcm_form_deflist}::
496	The standard pattern using AsciiDoc definition list syntax.
497	The anchor target appears on its own line, followed by an optional
498	{mcm_annotation}
499	then the term reference with `::` and definition text.
500	Use for standalone terms with self-contained meaning.
501	
502	[source,asciidoc]
503	----
504	[[term_anchor]]
505	//axl_voices axk_motif
506	{term_reference}::
507	Definition text following sentence-per-line rules.
508	----
509	
510	[[mcm_form_bullet]]
511	{mcm_form_bullet}::
512	A pattern for terms that belong to a parent structure or sequence.
513	Each term appears as a bullet item under its parent section.
514	The anchor target appears after the bullet marker, followed by an optional
515	{mcm_annotation}
516	then the term reference with definition prose.
517	Use for fields of a structure or steps in a procedure.
518	
519	[source,asciidoc]
520	----
521	* [[field_anchor]]
522	//axl_voices axr_member
523	{field_reference} defines the specific meaning within the parent structure.
524	----
525	
526	[[mcm_form_section]]
527	{mcm_form_section}::
528	A pattern where the section heading itself is the term.
529	The anchor target appears before the section marker, followed by an optional
530	{mcm_annotation}
531	then the section heading containing the term reference.
532	The section content constitutes the definition.
533	Use for terms whose meaning requires extended explanation or contains nested child terms.
534	
535	[source,asciidoc]
536	----
537	[[sequence_anchor]]
538	//axl_voices axo_sequence
539	=== {sequence_reference}
540	
541	The sequence executes these steps:
542	
543	* [[step_one_anchor]]
544	//axl_voices axo_step
545	First step description.
546	----

603	=== {mcm_category}, {mcm_variant}, and {mcm_domain} Usage
604	
605	The
606	{mcm_category},
607	{mcm_variant},
608	and
609	{mcm_domain}
610	work together to maintain precise term relationships and variations.

612	==== {mcm_category} Rules
613	
614	* Each
615	{mcm_category}
616	declares its scope in the
617	{mcm_mapping_section}
618	header
619	* {mcm_category_ies}
620	distinguish terms by conceptual role
621	* New
622	{mcm_category_ies}
623	require explicit declaration
624	
625	Example
626	{mcm_category}
627	declarations:
628	
629	[source,asciidoc]
630	----
631	// exn_: Noun concepts - entities and objects
632	// exv_: Verb concepts - actions and operations
633	----
634	
635	==== Variant Patterns
636	
637	Common
638	{mcm_variant_s}
639	follow consistent patterns:
640	
641	[source,asciidoc]
642	----
643	:exn_word:                   <<excm_word,Word>>
644	:exn_word_s:                 <<excm_word,Words>>
645	:exn_word_p:                 <<excm_word,Word's>>
646	:exv_act:                    <<excm_act,Act>>
647	:exv_act_s:                  <<excm_act,Acts>>
648	:exv_act_ed:                 <<excm_act,Acted>>
649	:exv_act_ing:                <<excm_act,Acting>>
650	----
651	
652	{mcm_normalization}
653	enforces consistent application of these patterns across the document.
654	
655	==== {mcm_domain} Pattern
656	
657	Each term definition establishes a unique anchor using snake case:
658	
659	[source,asciidoc]
660	----
661	// Domain declaration: excm_<canonical_term>
662	----
