664	== Task Lens Support
665	
666	{mcm_concept_model_s}
667	support focused work contexts through
668	{mcm_task_lens_es}
669	that emphasize relevant concepts while suppressing distractions.
670	See the
671	{mcm_task_lens_guide}
672	for compilation specifications and usage patterns.
673	
674	=== Task Lens Structure
675	
676	Each
677	{mcm_task_lens}
678	follows this pattern:
679	
680	[source,asciidoc]
681	----
682	=== [Task Lens Name]
683	
684	**Purpose**: [One sentence describing the work this lens enables]
685	
686	**Gestalt**: [Metaphorical description guiding term interpretation]
687	
688	**Core Activation**:
689	
690	* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
691	* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
692	
693	**Expanded Activation**:
694	
695	* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
696	* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
697	
698	**Suppressed Activation**:
699	
700	* List of {linked_term_s} to remain dormant
701	* Additional suppression patterns
702	
703	**Critical Relationships**:
704	
705	* {term_a} ↔ {term_b}: [What aspect matters]
706	* {term_c} → {term_d}: [Specific mechanism focus]
707	----
708	
709	Multiple
710	{mcm_task_lens_es}
711	may share terms but apply different
712	{mcm_focal_depth_s}
713	and aspect emphasis based on their purpose.
714	
715	=== Blank Line Exception for Task Lenses
716	
717	Despite the general document rule against
718	{mcm_blank_line_s}
719	around list items,
720	{mcm_task_lens}
721	sections require specific blank lines for valid asciidoc parsing:
722	
723	* One blank line after each `**Section**:` header before its list
724	* One blank line between major sections
725	* No blank lines between list items within a section
726	
727	This exception exists because asciidoc requires blank lines before bulleted lists to parse correctly, and the structured nature of
728	{mcm_task_lens_es}
729	makes the blank line placement predictable and consistent.
730	
731	== Actor Definitions
732	
733	[[mcm_editor]]
734	{mcm_editor}::
735	The human originator of
736	{mcm_concept_model_s}
737	and constraints.
738	The
739	{mcm_editor}
740	guides document evolution, establishes patterns, and directs transformations while maintaining conceptual integrity.
741	
742	[[mcm_digital_mind]]
743	{mcm_digital_mind}::
744	An agent, typically an LLM, transforming
745	{mcm_document_s}
746	under
747	{mcm_editor}
748	direction and document constraints.
749	The
750	{mcm_digital_mind}
751	performs well-defined
752	{mcm_curation_action_s}
753	while preserving document patterns and relationships.
754	
755	== {mcm_curation_action_s}
756	
757	[[mcm_curation_action]]
758	{mcm_curation_action}::
759	A process executed by a
760	{mcm_digital_mind}
761	to adjust a
762	{mcm_document}
763	as directed by the
764	{mcm_editor}.
765	Each
766	{mcm_curation_action}
767	has a specific focus and constraints, modifying only those aspects of the
768	{mcm_document}
769	that fall within its defined scope.
770	
771	[[mcm_normalization]]
772	{mcm_normalization}::
773	A
774	{mcm_curation_action}
775	that brings a
776	{mcm_document}
777	to canonical form through two phases.
778	
779	*Phase 1: Text Normalization*
780	
781	Applies consistent formatting throughout the
782	{mcm_document}
783	body:
784	
785	* One sentence per line
786	* Each
787	{mcm_linked_term}
788	on its own line
789	* Commas and punctuation attached to preceding element
790	* {mcm_blank_line_s}
791	only between paragraphs and definition entries
792	* Preserve source code block contents without modification
793	
794	*Phase 2: Mapping Section Normalization*
795	
796	Maintains strict structural integrity of the
797	{mcm_mapping_section}:
798	
799	* Preserve
800	{mcm_category}
801	comment headers as
802	{mcm_category_group}
803	delimiters
804	* Within each
805	{mcm_category_group},
806	align `<<` to the smallest multiple of 10 columns that accommodates the longest
807	{mcm_attribute_reference}
808	* Maintain alphabetical ordering by display text within each
809	{mcm_category_group}
810	* One
811	{mcm_attribute_reference}
812	per line
813	
814	[[mcm_category_group]]
815	{mcm_category_group}::
816	A contiguous block of
817	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
818	in the
819	{mcm_mapping_section}
820	preceded by a category comment header.
821	Each
822	{mcm_category_group}
823	maintains independent column alignment,
824	allowing terms to be added without affecting other groups.
825	
826	[[mcm_demotion_analysis]]
827	{mcm_demotion_analysis}::
828	A
829	{mcm_curation_action}
830	that identifies
831	{mcm_linked_term_s}
832	whose formal status may no longer justify their maintenance overhead.
833	It suggests candidates for demotion to
834	{mcm_fundamental_text}
835	based on criteria such as:
836	* Rare usage outside their own definition
837	* Lack of meaningful variation requiring
838	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
839	* Minimal role in expressing relationships between other terms
840	* Terms whose precise meaning has become self-evident in context
841	
842	[[mcm_link_validation]]
843	{mcm_link_validation}::
844	A
845	{mcm_curation_action}
846	that verifies the mechanical integrity of the
847	{mcm_document_p}
848	linking system without modification:
849	
850	* Reports
851	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
852	that lack corresponding anchors
853	* Reports anchors that lack corresponding
854	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
855	* Validates
856	{mcm_domain}
857	format compliance
858	* Identifies malformed link syntax
859	
860	This action produces a diagnostic report for
861	{mcm_editor}
862	review.
863	Repairs are performed manually or through targeted
864	{mcm_curation_action_s}.
865	
866	[[mcm_promotion_analysis]]
867	{mcm_promotion_analysis}:: 
868	A
869	{mcm_curation_action}
870	that identifies frequently occurring or conceptually significant elements in
871	{mcm_fundamental_text}
872	that warrant promotion to
873	{mcm_linked_term}
874	status.
875	It suggests candidates based on:
876	* Frequency in definitions of other
877	{mcm_linked_term_s}
878	* Use in expressing core conceptual relationships
879	* Occurrence in multiple distinct contexts
880	* Words whose precise meaning needs maintenance across document evolution
881	
882	[[mcm_term_matching]]
883	{mcm_term_matching}::
884	A
885	{mcm_curation_action}
886	that identifies instances where concepts corresponding to existing
887	{mcm_linked_term_s}
888	appear in text without proper linking:
889	* Identifies important terms that warrant linking
890	* Proposes what should be linked
891	
892	[[mcm_validation_summary]]
893	{mcm_validation_summary}::
894	An advisory report produced by
895	{mcm_link_validation}
896	that documents the state of the
897	{mcm_document}
898	without making modifications.
899	The
900	{mcm_validation_summary}
901	enables the
902	{mcm_editor}
903	to identify issues requiring attention.

=== {mcm_category}, {mcm_variant}, and {mcm_domain} Usage

The

and
{mcm_domain}
work together to maintain precise term relationships and variations.

==== {mcm_category} Rules

* Each
{mcm_category}
declares its scope in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
header
* {mcm_category_ies}
distinguish terms by conceptual role
* New
{mcm_category_ies}
require explicit declaration

Example
{mcm_category}
declarations:

[source,asciidoc]
----
// exn_: Noun concepts - entities and objects
// exv_: Verb concepts - actions and operations
----

==== Variant Patterns

Common
{mcm_variant_s}
follow consistent patterns:

[source,asciidoc]
----
:exn_word:                   <<excm_word,Word>>
:exn_word_s:                 <<excm_word,Words>>
:exn_word_p:                 <<excm_word,Word's>>
:exv_act:                    <<excm_act,Act>>
:exv_act_s:                  <<excm_act,Acts>>
:exv_act_ed:                 <<excm_act,Acted>>
:exv_act_ing:                <<excm_act,Acting>>
----

{mcm_normalization}
enforces consistent application of these patterns across the document.

==== {mcm_domain} Pattern

Each term definition establishes a unique anchor using snake case:

[source,asciidoc]
----
// Domain declaration: excm_<canonical_term>
----

== Task Lens Support

{mcm_concept_model_s}
support focused work contexts through
{mcm_task_lens_es}
that emphasize relevant concepts while suppressing distractions.
See the
{mcm_task_lens_guide}
for compilation specifications and usage patterns.

=== Task Lens Structure

Each
{mcm_task_lens}
follows this pattern:

[source,asciidoc]
----
=== [Task Lens Name]

**Purpose**: [One sentence describing the work this lens enables]

**Gestalt**: [Metaphorical description guiding term interpretation]

**Core Activation**:

* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]
* {linked_term} - [Aspect emphasis if needed]

**Expanded Activation**:

* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]
* {linked_term} - [Reduced detail focus]

**Suppressed Activation**:

* List of {linked_term_s} to remain dormant
* Additional suppression patterns

**Critical Relationships**:

* {term_a} ↔ {term_b}: [What aspect matters]
* {term_c} → {term_d}: [Specific mechanism focus]
----

Multiple
{mcm_task_lens_es}
may share terms but apply different
{mcm_focal_depth_s}
and aspect emphasis based on their purpose.

=== Blank Line Exception for Task Lenses

Despite the general document rule against
{mcm_blank_line_s}
around list items,
{mcm_task_lens}
sections require specific blank lines for valid asciidoc parsing:

* One blank line after each `**Section**:` header before its list
* One blank line between major sections
* No blank lines between list items within a section

This exception exists because asciidoc requires blank lines before bulleted lists to parse correctly, and the structured nature of
{mcm_task_lens_es}
makes the blank line placement predictable and consistent.

== Actor Definitions

[[mcm_editor]]
{mcm_editor}::
The human originator of
{mcm_concept_model_s}
and constraints.
The
{mcm_editor}
guides document evolution, establishes patterns, and directs transformations while maintaining conceptual integrity.

[[mcm_digital_mind]]
{mcm_digital_mind}::
An agent, typically an LLM, transforming
{mcm_document_s}
under
{mcm_editor}
direction and document constraints.
The
{mcm_digital_mind}
performs well-defined
{mcm_curation_action_s}
while preserving document patterns and relationships.

== {mcm_curation_action_s}

[[mcm_curation_action]]
{mcm_curation_action}::
A process executed by a
{mcm_digital_mind}
to adjust a
{mcm_document}
as directed by the
{mcm_editor}.
Each
{mcm_curation_action}
has a specific focus and constraints, modifying only those aspects of the
{mcm_document}
that fall within its defined scope.

[[mcm_normalization]]
{mcm_normalization}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that brings a
{mcm_document}
to canonical form through two phases.

*Phase 1: Text Normalization*

Applies consistent formatting throughout the
{mcm_document}
body:

* One sentence per line
* Each
{mcm_linked_term}
on its own line
* Commas and punctuation attached to preceding element
* {mcm_blank_line_s}
only between paragraphs and definition entries
* Preserve source code block contents without modification

*Phase 2: Mapping Section Normalization*

Maintains strict structural integrity of the
{mcm_mapping_section}:

* Preserve
{mcm_category}
comment headers as
{mcm_category_group}
delimiters
* Within each
{mcm_category_group},
align `<<` to the smallest multiple of 10 columns that accommodates the longest
{mcm_attribute_reference}
* Maintain alphabetical ordering by display text within each
{mcm_category_group}
* One
{mcm_attribute_reference}
per line

[[mcm_category_group]]
{mcm_category_group}::
A contiguous block of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
in the
{mcm_mapping_section}
preceded by a category comment header.
Each
{mcm_category_group}
maintains independent column alignment,
allowing terms to be added without affecting other groups.

[[mcm_demotion_analysis]]
{mcm_demotion_analysis}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies
{mcm_linked_term_s}
whose formal status may no longer justify their maintenance overhead.
It suggests candidates for demotion to
{mcm_fundamental_text}
based on criteria such as:
* Rare usage outside their own definition
* Lack of meaningful variation requiring
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
* Minimal role in expressing relationships between other terms
* Terms whose precise meaning has become self-evident in context

[[mcm_link_validation]]
{mcm_link_validation}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that verifies the mechanical integrity of the
{mcm_document_p}
linking system without modification:

* Reports
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
that lack corresponding anchors
* Reports anchors that lack corresponding
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
* Validates
{mcm_domain}
format compliance
* Identifies malformed link syntax

This action produces a diagnostic report for
{mcm_editor}
review.
Repairs are performed manually or through targeted
{mcm_curation_action_s}.

[[mcm_promotion_analysis]]
{mcm_promotion_analysis}:: 
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies frequently occurring or conceptually significant elements in
{mcm_fundamental_text}
that warrant promotion to
{mcm_linked_term}
status.
It suggests candidates based on:
* Frequency in definitions of other
{mcm_linked_term_s}
* Use in expressing core conceptual relationships
* Occurrence in multiple distinct contexts
* Words whose precise meaning needs maintenance across document evolution

[[mcm_term_matching]]
{mcm_term_matching}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
that identifies instances where concepts corresponding to existing
{mcm_linked_term_s}
appear in text without proper linking:
* Identifies important terms that warrant linking
* Proposes what should be linked

[[mcm_validation_summary]]
{mcm_validation_summary}::
An advisory report produced by
{mcm_link_validation}
that documents the state of the
{mcm_document}
without making modifications.
The
{mcm_validation_summary}
enables the
{mcm_editor}
to identify issues requiring attention.

== Word Selection

Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
this section governs the right half — the word itself.
The rules below bind the word part of every

content.text:
MCM read whole, tail included. Three answers, then the proposal set.

## 1. The `//axl_voices` deprecation — no, it is not noted anywhere

AXLA's `axl_annotation` definition still teaches `//axl_voices motif [dims]` as *the* format, with examples; MCM's three form-pattern examples (`mcm_form_deflist/bullet/section`) model the same old form. Meanwhile the field has moved: the lenses annotate direct (`//axvo_procedure`). Nothing marks the divergence — hence the recurring stumble. Proposed clarification, to land in this batch:

- **AXLA**: the leading relationship token is now implicit — `//<motif> [dims]` is the authored form, *voices* the default relationship. The explicit `//axl_voices ...` form is **legacy: readable, never authored anew** (un-scrubbed instances stand until remint tooling sweeps them). The `axl_voices` quoin itself survives — the *relationship* is real; only its token in the annotation line retires.
- **MCM**: modernize the three form-pattern example blocks to the direct form. `mcm_annotation` itself is already agnostic ("grammar… defined by the referencing system") and needs nothing.

## 2. Heat: **₣Bd** — the MCM doctrine heat; Word Selection landed there. Notches go to it.

## 3. The quoin proposals

The meld I found while drinking: MCM's meta-vocabulary is already an asterism of physical-craft gestalts where *the name is the gestalt* — and **quoin already seeds a masonry family**. A wall's anatomy mirrors the exposure doctrine exactly: dressed facing stone for what's seen, rough fill for what isn't, quoins locking the corners. The asterism does structural work — which is MCM's own test for one.

| Quoin | Concept | The gestalt |
|---|---|---|
| `mcm_ashlar` | operator vocabulary (T1) | the dressed facing stone — finished finely *because* it faces out. Binds: the six rules + fair-faced + coffer + specimen |
| `mcm_hearting` | interior names (T3) | the wall-core fill — structural, unseen, takes no dressing. Codifies your "let go internally" as doctrine, not neglect |
| — (T2) | spec-controlled middle | **no new quoin** — T2 *is* the existing quoin estate under Word Selection; the unmarked case |
| `mcm_fair_faced` | first-contact comprehension | masonry: a surface finished so it needs no render coat. An ashlar word must be fair-faced — actionable at first encounter, no glossary coat. Note the family carrying the rule: ashlar *is* fair-faced stone |
| `mcm_coffer` | the bounded T1 budget | treasury chest, with the architectural coffer as second resonance. Each ashlar mint draws from it; spec precision collapses into fewer operator words when the distinction doesn't change operator action |
| `mcm_specimen` | the designated public registry | the type-foundry specimen sheet — letterpress, beside quoin and sprue. RB's specimen is the README glossary; a mint isn't done until it's on the sheet |
| `mcm_cold_probe` | the test (curation action) | named plainly like its siblings (normalization, link_validation), echoing the project's existing cold-probe usage: one sentence of working context to a fresh reader; their stated expectation is the verdict |
| `axd_ashlar` | AXLA dimension term | the definition-site marker, authored direct-form — and the lint hook: an `axd_ashlar` quoin missing from the specimen is a detectable defect |

Traffic weighting folds into ashlar's definition as prose; the **primacy preamble** opens the section as prose (citing Load-Bearing Complexity), unquoined until something needs to cite it by name.

All words pending the grep gate at write time — `specimen` is the one I'd watch (test-specimen prose may collide); the masonry words will land clean. And the recursion stands as a feature: every one of these mints must pass the section it extends.

Remaining questions: just one — T2 left unmarked is a deliberate asymmetry (mark only the poles); confirm that sits right with you, and I'll draft the edits.

content: We're codifying your naming doctrine (exposure tiers) into MCM/AXLA before the vocabulary pace. Quoin proposals are on the table: ashlar, hearting, fair_faced, coffer, specimen, cold_probe, axd_ashlar. Next: your approval, then I draft the edits under ₣Bd. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text:
Let me lay it out plainly.

We have three kinds of names. Words your users see. Words your specs control. Words inside the code that nobody ceremonies over. I proposed a new name for the top (ashlar) and the bottom (hearting), and nothing for the middle. You're being asked to bless that gap.

Here's why I think the gap is right, said simply: **the middle tier already has a name. It's "quoin."** A spec-controlled word is just a quoin — the thing MCM has governed all along. I wasn't proposing two names out of three; I was proposing names for the two things that *didn't* have one yet.

And that reframe fixes something slightly wrong in how I drew it earlier. These aren't three separate bins you sort words into. It's a ladder of increasing care:

- **hearting** — not catalogued at all. Prefix rules only. Deliberately left loose.
- **quoin** — catalogued: mapping entry, anchor, definition, the six word-selection rules.
- **ashlar** — a quoin *that also faces the user*. Everything quoin demands, plus fair-faced, plus the coffer, plus the specimen sheet.

Ashlar isn't a sibling of quoin — it's a property stacked on top of one. Depot is a quoin *and* ashlar. `zrbgi_poll` is hearting. The middle rung is the default state of every catalogued word, so naming it again would mint a word with no job: there's no sentence you'd ever need it for that "quoin" doesn't already say.

One honest wrinkle, since defaults matter: if unmarked means "just a quoin," then *forgetting* to mark a word ashlar fails quiet — the word skips the stricter tests and nobody is warned. The lint hook catches the reverse mistake (marked ashlar but missing from the specimen), not this one. I don't think that justifies a middle-tier name — the hole is about forgetting a mark, and a third name wouldn't remind anyone. The real guard is the specimen review habit: when a word shows up in user output, ask "is this on the sheet?"

So my answer to my own question: the asymmetry sits right because it isn't really asymmetric — every rung has a name, and the middle one was named years ago. If that lands for you, I'll draft.

message.content: please draft now, with my thanks.  After, remind me of anything else we might want to stash in paddock or paces or MCM/AXLA/CLAUDE.md/claude-*.md from this chat?  I think I am close to retiring this and moving on.  The spike is in progress now.

do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
Applicability of
{axd_dimension_s}
depends on serialization format context.

[[axd_dimension]]
{axd_dimension}::
A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
{axl_annotation}.
{axd_dimension_s}
appear after the primary
{axl_motif}
in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`

=== Value Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).

[[axd_required]]
{axd_required}::
Member or argument must be present.
Absence triggers validation error or
{axc_fatal}.

[[axd_optional]]
{axd_optional}::
Member or argument may be omitted.
Absence is valid and handled gracefully.

[[axd_nullable]]
{axd_nullable}::
Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.

== Word Selection

Vocabulary is the primary interface of every system the
{mcm_editor}
choreographs:
names are how intent survives across minds and sessions,
and the specification machinery around them is scaffolding in their service.
A name that fails its reader fails the system,
however sound the code beneath it.

Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
this section governs the right half — the word itself.

Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
Model-side rationale: `Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.

=== Exposure

Word-selection care scales with who must read the name.
Every persistent name sits on a ladder of increasing dressing:

* {mcm_hearting} — interior names, uncatalogued, governed by prefix discipline alone.
* A
{mcm_quoin}
— the catalogued default; the rules above bind.
* {mcm_ashlar} — a
{mcm_quoin}
that faces the operator; everything below binds, plus the rules at its definition.

The ladder is deliberate at both ends:
{mcm_hearting}
is permission to leave interior names plain,
granted so that care can concentrate where reading is costly.
Misclassification fails quiet in one direction —
a word that should be
{mcm_ashlar}
but is unmarked skips the stricter tests —
so when a word surfaces in operator output, ask whether it belongs on the
{mcm_broadside}.

[[mcm_ashlar]]
{mcm_ashlar}::
A
{mcm_quoin}
the operator must trigger, observe in output or errors, or reason about system state with.
The dressed facing stone — finished finely because it faces out.
Beyond the rules above, an
{mcm_ashlar}
must be
{mcm_fair_faced},
draws from the
{mcm_coffer},
and is not minted until it appears on the project's
{mcm_broadside}.
Scrutiny scales with contact frequency:
a word the operator meets daily outranks a founding-ceremony word,
and both outrank any spec-interior
{mcm_quoin}.
Verbs name what the operator does or sees, never the mechanism beneath.
Words appearing in failure output are
{mcm_ashlar_s}
even when the operator never invokes them.

[[mcm_hearting]]
{mcm_hearting}::
The wall-core fill — structural, unseen, taking no dressing.
Interior names governed by prefix discipline alone:
locals, private helpers, intermediate artifacts no operator and no spec must read.
{mcm_hearting}
is a deliberate grant of plainness, not neglect;
its existence is what lets
{mcm_ashlar}
care stay affordable.

[[mcm_fair_faced]]
{mcm_fair_faced}::
The property an
{mcm_ashlar}
must hold: finished so it needs no render coat.
An operator meeting the word for the first time, mid-ceremony,
must act correctly without a glossary lookup.
Rare enough to land clean in the grep gate,
common enough to guess warm —
the deliberate tightening of rare-but-real at the wall's face.
The
{mcm_cold_probe}
is its test.

[[mcm_coffer]]
{mcm_coffer}::
The bounded budget of
{mcm_ashlar_s}
an operator can be asked to hold.
Each mint spends from it;
spec-grain precision collapses into fewer operator words wherever the distinction does not change what the operator does.
The fine grain lives on in
{mcm_quoin_s};
the
{mcm_coffer}
governs only the wall's face.

[[mcm_broadside]]
{mcm_broadside}::
The single public sheet where a project posts its
{mcm_ashlar}
vocabulary.
Each project designates exactly one;
presence on the
{mcm_broadside}
is the registry contract —
an
{mcm_ashlar}
mint is incomplete until its entry exists.

[[mcm_cold_probe]]
{mcm_cold_probe}::
A
{mcm_curation_action}
testing
{mcm_fair_faced}:
present each candidate to a fresh reader in one sentence of working context;
the reader states what they expect the word to do or mean.
The stated expectation is the verdict —
mismatch fails the candidate,
and no advocacy by the minter is admitted.

input.old_string:
- **Mint into an asterism** (`mcm_asterism`): join a coherent metaphor family with an audible register (equestrian, ecclesiastical, diplomatic, civic).

input.new_string:
- **Mint into an asterism** (`mcm_asterism`): join a coherent metaphor family with an audible register (equestrian, ecclesiastical, diplomatic, civic).
- **Exposure ladder** (`mcm_ashlar`/`mcm_hearting`): hearting (interior, prefix-only) → quoin (catalogued default) → ashlar (operator-facing). An ashlar must be fair-faced (first-contact actionable; `mcm_cold_probe` tests it), draws from the coffer (bounded operator vocabulary), and registers on the project's broadside (RB's broadside is the README glossary). ## Concept Model Kit Configuration

Concept Model Kit (CMK) is installed for managing concept model documents.

**Configuration:**
- Lenses directory: `lenses`
- Kit path: `Tools/cmk/README.md`
- Upstream remote: `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM`

### CMK File Acronyms

| Acronym | File | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| **MCM** | `cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc` | Meta Concept Model — the spec governing all concept model documents |
| **AXLA** | `cmk/vov_veiled/AXLA-Lexicon.adoc` | Axial Lexicon — shared vocabulary of motifs reusable across concept models |
| **AXMCM** | `cmk/vov_veiled/AXMCM-ClaudeMarkConceptMemo.md` | ClaudeMark concept memo |
| **ACG** | `cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md` | Allocation Coding Guide — source-side complement to MCM: reference the home, don't recreate (values→constants, concepts→quoin-refs). Veiled/proprietary move-catalog; guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG. |

### MCM Vocabulary

| Term | Prefix | Meaning |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Quoin** | `mcm_` | A formal identifier with full cataloguing: attribute reference in the mapping section, anchor at the definition site, and definition text. The cornerstone — addressable from anywhere, tracked across the constellation. |
| **Mapping Section** | `mcm_` | The block of attribute references at the top of a document, between `tag::mapping-section[]` markers. Where quoins are registered. |
| **Concept Model** | `mcm_` | A unified constellation of linked terms whose relationships give meaning to a domain. Manifested as an AsciiDoc document following MCM patterns. |
| **Category** | `mcm_` | The prefix group (e.g., `bzsn_`, `bzsdp_`, `mcm_`) that scopes a set of related quoins. |
| **Variant** | `mcm_` | Suffix modifiers on attribute references: `_s` plural, `_p` possessive, `_ed` past, `_ing` progressive. |
| **Annotation** | `mcm_` | An AsciiDoc comment line carrying structured metadata as space-separated prefix terms. Position determines role: relationship first, primary motif second, dimensions following. Example: `//axl_voices axk_premise`. |

### AXLA Vocabulary

| Term | Prefix | Meaning |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Motif** | `axl_` | A reusable conceptual pattern from AXLA that a quoin can voice. The shared vocabulary across concept models. |
| **Voicing** | `axl_` | An annotation at a definition site declaring which AXLA motif a quoin instantiates (e.g., `// axl_voices axk_premise`). |
| **Premise** | `axk_` | A motif representing a declared design constraint that bounds system complexity by stating what the system will not handle. |
| **Definition Site** | `axl_` | The location where a quoin's `[[anchor]]` and definition text appear. Where voicings are declared. |

### Concept Model Patterns

- **Linked Terms**: `{category_term}` — references defined vocabulary
- **Attribute References**: `:category_term: <<anchor,Display Text>>` — in mapping section
- **Anchors**: `[[anchor_name]]` — definition targets
- **Annotations**: `//axl_voices axk_premise` — prefix-discriminated comment lines (no space after `//`, letter distinguishes from regular comments)

### Minting Guidance

Before introducing new quoin prefixes in an MCM document, consult the CLAUDE.md "Quoin Sub-Letter Discipline" subsection and minting memo Pattern J (`Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`). The rule: uniform `prefixXY_word` shape, hard 2-letter ceiling, within-domain Y monosemy, documented sub-letter legend in the spec's mapping section.

**Available commands:**
- `/cma-prep-pr` - Prepare upstream contribution

For full MCM specification, see `Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc`.

file.content:
See minting memo Pattern J (RBS0/JJS0 empirical study) for evidence.

### Word Selection Discipline (MCM "Word Selection")

Prefix rules govern the left half of a name; these govern the word itself. When minting any persistent name — quoin word-parts, verbs, nouns, colophon words:

- **Semantic uniqueness** repo-wide: one meaning per word, ever.
- **Vocabulary isolation**: no *trodden words* (`mcm_trodden_word`) — vocabulary heavy in ambient software prose (open, close, run, lock, task, grant) is disqualified regardless of fit.
- **Grep gate**: repo-wide grep must land clean before adoption.
- **Rare but real**: distinctive words whose true meaning does semantic work; concrete over abstract; never abbreviations.
- **Mint into an asterism** (`mcm_asterism`): join a coherent metaphor family with an audible register (equestrian, ecclesiastical, diplomatic, civic).

Full doctrine: MCM "Word Selection". Constrains births, not the living.

### Minting Workflow

| **Quoin** | `mcm_` | A formal identifier with full cataloguing: attribute reference in the mapping section, anchor at the definition site, and definition text. The cornerstone — addressable from anywhere, tracked across the constellation. |
| **Mapping Section** | `mcm_` | The block of attribute references at the top of a document, between `tag::mapping-section[]` markers. Where quoins are registered. |
| **Concept Model** | `mcm_` | A unified constellation of linked terms whose relationships give meaning to a domain. Manifested as an AsciiDoc document following MCM patterns. |
| **Category** | `mcm_` | The prefix group (e.g., `bzsn_`, `bzsdp_`, `mcm_`) that scopes a set of related quoins. |
| **Variant** | `mcm_` | Suffix modifiers on attribute references: `_s` plural, `_p` possessive, `_ed` past, `_ing` progressive. |
| **Annotation** | `mcm_` | An AsciiDoc comment line carrying structured metadata as space-separated prefix terms. Position determines role: primary motif first, dimensions following; the voices relationship is implicit. Example: `//axk_premise`. Legacy `//axl_voices …` form is read-only — never author it anew. |

### AXLA Vocabulary

| Term | Prefix | Meaning |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Motif** | `axl_` | A reusable conceptual pattern from AXLA that a quoin can voice. The shared vocabulary across concept models. |
| **Voicing** | `axl_` | An annotation at a definition site declaring which AXLA motif a quoin instantiates (e.g., `//axk_premise`). |
| **Premise** | `axk_` | A motif representing a declared design constraint that bounds system complexity by stating what the system will not handle. |
| **Definition Site** | `axl_` | The location where a quoin's `[[anchor]]` and definition text appear. Where voicings are declared. |

### Concept Model Patterns

- **Linked Terms**: `{category_term}` — references defined vocabulary
- **Attribute References**: `:category_term: <<anchor,Display Text>>` — in mapping section
- **Anchors**: `[[anchor_name]]` — definition targets
- **Annotations**: `//axk_premise` — prefix-discriminated comment lines (no space after `//`, letter distinguishes from regular comments); primary motif first, `axd_` dimensions follow, voices relationship implicit. Legacy `//axl_voices …` form is read-only.

### Minting Guidance

Before introducing new quoin prefixes in an MCM document, consult the CLAUDE.md "Quoin Sub-Letter Discipline" subsection and minting memo Pattern J (`Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`). The rule: uniform `prefixXY_word` shape, hard 2-letter ceiling, within-domain Y monosemy, documented sub-letter legend in the spec's mapping section.

**Available commands:**
- `/cma-prep-pr` - Prepare upstream contribution

For full MCM specification, see `Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc`.

Notched to ₣Bd (180c2497). The full batch:

- **MCM** — Word Selection opens with the primacy preamble; new `=== Exposure` ladder with five quoins (`mcm_ashlar`, `mcm_hearting`, `mcm_fair_faced`, `mcm_coffer`, `mcm_broadside`) and `mcm_cold_probe` as a curation action; all four form-pattern examples modernized to direct annotations. `specimen` failed its own grep gate (8 live exemplar-sense uses, one inside the very section) — the registry quoin is **broadside**, which landed clean.

- **CLAUDE.md + claude-cmk-core.md** — exposure-ladder echoes, your eviction-then-reuse ruling recorded beside semantic uniqueness, and the legacy annotation form purged from both context surfaces — which were themselves teaching the old form to every fresh session, and were likely the recurring stumble's actual root.

## What's left to stash, then you can retire this

1. **The vocabulary pace** — deliberately unslated until the spike lands. When slated, its docket cites: the ashlar doctrine, the eviction-then-reuse ruling, RB's broadside = README glossary, and the four shape questions (user-facing admission-verb count, citizen survival, shared sign-in word incl. payor, whether the generic office noun is ashlar at all). I can slate it at the post-spike groom — nothing needed now.
2. **One-line paddock touch, optional** — the paddock's vocabulary Open item predates today's doctrine; pointing it at `mcm_ashlar`/exposure ladder is a small edit I'd fold into the next groom rather than churn a commit now.
3. **Optional itch** — CMK lint tooling for the `axd_ashlar`-without-broadside-entry defect; the hook is declared in AXLA, nothing enforces it yet.
4. **Push when convenient** — the spike clone will want today's commits (paddock, decision memo, MCM doctrine), and you'll want its findings back.

Everything else from this chat — office architecture, canon supersession, custody layers, retriever posture, multi-IdP cinch, test-rig open — is already banked in the paddock, the decision memo, or the beta evidence memos. This was a genuinely good run, Brad: a credential model died, a better one took its place, and the naming doctrine that will dress it got built on the way out. Retire it well.
